description:
You are a Performance Engineer investigating a potential software regression after an Object-Relational Mapping (ORM) library update. The database team reports elevated RDS CPU utilization, but the product team claims it's just due to a marketing campaign driving more traffic. You must build an 'Efficiency Index' dashboard that calculates requests served per 1% of DB CPU to prove if a regression occurred regardless of traffic volume.

Create and save a Graphite dashboard named 'ORM Efficiency Analysis' containing four graphs with the following exact titles and targets:
- 'Raw Traffic Rate': nonNegativeDerivative(servers.load_balancer.requests.count)
- 'Database CPU': servers.rds_database.cpu.utilization
- 'Efficiency Index': divideSeries(nonNegativeDerivative(servers.load_balancer.requests.count), servers.rds_database.cpu.utilization)
- 'Efficiency Degradation': compare the current efficiency index target divideSeries(nonNegativeDerivative(servers.load_balancer.requests.count), servers.rds_database.cpu.utilization) against the previous day's data using timeShift(divideSeries(nonNegativeDerivative(servers.load_balancer.requests.count), servers.rds_database.cpu.utilization), "1d").
